 {"id":"gt-8tmz.23","title":"Compile-time vs runtime cooking","description":"Distinguish two cooking modes: 1) Compile-time: produces proto with variable placeholders, for modeling/estimation/contractor handoff. 2) Runtime: produces mol with real issue IDs. Compile-time lets you pre-decompose work for untrusted executors or planning purposes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:45:50.5263-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T18:45:50.5263-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.23","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:45:50.527834-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Propagate terminology to all relevant docs: - molecule-algebra.md (done) - molecular-chemistry.md (done) - vision.md (done) - propulsion-principle.md - sling-design.md - Any agent templates that reference cooking/instantiation","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:18:26.167525-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T19:18:26.167525-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.24","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:18:26.16795-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.25","title":"Implement bd bond command for artifact-level composition","description":"Implement the artifact-level bond operator distinct from source-level rigging. Bond operates on cooked protos, mols, and wisps. See rig-cook-run.md for the full bond table semantics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:18:27.245107-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T19:18:27.245107-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.25","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:18:27.246957-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-8tmz.26","title":"Direct formula-to-wisp instantiation (skip proto)","description":"For massive ephemeral workflows (e.g., million-step Towers of Hanoi), allow:

bd wisp towers-of-hanoi --var disks=20

This would cook + wisp in one step, never materializing the proto. Useful when:
- The proto would be huge (1M+ steps)
- The work is ephemeral (wisp, not mol)
- You don't need the intermediate artifact

Implementation: streaming cook that emits directly to wisp storage.

Related: towers-of-hanoi.formula.yaml demonstrates the use case.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:26:53.377902-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T19:26:53.377902-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.26","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:26:53.378307-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.3","title":"Expansion operators: expand and map (macros)","description":"Implement macro-style expansion operators:
- expand(target, template) - apply template to single step
- map(molecule, template) - apply template to all matching steps

Templates use {target} substitution for step references.
Rule of Five is the canonical example.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:31.472199-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:31.472199-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:31.472662-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:49.809887-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:48:18.717567-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.4","title":"Control flow: loop, gate, branch","description":"Implement control flow operators:
- loop N { body } - fixed iteration
- loop until COND { body } - conditional iteration with max bound
- gate COND { body } - wait for condition
- branch from/steps/join - parallel paths that rejoin

Conditions evaluated mechanically (step status, output fields).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:33.194896-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:33.194896-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.4","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:33.196543-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.4","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:49.985527-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.5","title":"Aspects: AOP cross-cutting concerns","description":"Implement aspect-oriented composition:
- Define aspects with pointcuts and advice
- Apply aspects at bond time: bd bond mol --with-aspect security
- Pointcuts use glob patterns to match join points

Enables security-audit, logging, etc. as reusable concerns.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:34.128562-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:34.128562-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.5","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:34.130564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.5","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:49.898125-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.5","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:48:18.802195-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"gt-o3is","title":"gt sling pinToHook doesn't set pinned boolean field","description":"## Updated Root Cause Analysis (2025-12-23)

The issue is **NOT** in gt as originally thought. It's in the beads (bd) codebase.

### What happens:
1. `bd pin` correctly sets pinned=1 in SQLite
2. Any subsequent `bd` command (even `bd show`) resets pinned to 0
3. This happens even with `--no-auto-import` and `--sandbox` flags

### Evidence:
```bash
$ bd --no-daemon pin gt-k08o --for=max
📌 Pinned gt-k08o to max's hook

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'
gt-k08o|1   # ← Correct immediately after pin

$ bd --no-daemon --no-auto-import show gt-k08o
[shows issue without pinned field]

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'  
gt-k08o|0   # ← WRONG\! bd show overwrote it
```

### Where to look:
The bug is likely in one of these beads code paths:
- Some import/hydration running despite --no-auto-import
- WAL mode not flushing before subsequent reads
- Multi-repo or redirect handling corrupting pinned field

### Workaround:
The handoff bead attachment mechanism (AttachMolecule) works correctly.
The pinned field is cosmetic for `bd hook` visibility only.
gt sling correctly uses AttachMolecule for work assignment.

### Next steps:
Create a beads issue to fix this properly in the bd codebase.
This is not a gt issue.","notes":"Created beads bug: bd-phtv in ~/gt/beads","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:41:12.668958-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:32:43.506087-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-o3is","title":"gt sling pinToHook doesn't set pinned boolean field","description":"## Updated Root Cause Analysis (2025-12-23)

The issue is **NOT** in gt as originally thought. It's in the beads (bd) codebase.

### What happens:
1. `bd pin` correctly sets pinned=1 in SQLite
2. Any subsequent `bd` command (even `bd show`) resets pinned to 0
3. This happens even with `--no-auto-import` and `--sandbox` flags

### Evidence:
```bash
$ bd --no-daemon pin gt-k08o --for=max
📌 Pinned gt-k08o to max's hook

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'
gt-k08o|1   # ← Correct immediately after pin

$ bd --no-daemon --no-auto-import show gt-k08o
[shows issue without pinned field]

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'  
gt-k08o|0   # ← WRONG\! bd show overwrote it
```

### Where to look:
The bug is likely in one of these beads code paths:
- Some import/hydration running despite --no-auto-import
- WAL mode not flushing before subsequent reads
- Multi-repo or redirect handling corrupting pinned field

### Workaround:
The handoff bead attachment mechanism (AttachMolecule) works correctly.
The pinned field is cosmetic for `bd hook` visibility only.
gt sling correctly uses AttachMolecule for work assignment.

### Next steps:
Create a beads issue to fix this properly in the bd codebase.
This is not a gt issue.","notes":"Created beads bug: bd-phtv in ~/gt/beads","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:41:12.668958-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T19:26:56.492585-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-o40t","title":"gt sling --force: return displaced work to ready pool","description":"When slinging with --force to an agent with occupied hook, the displaced molecule should be returned to the ready pool rather than silently orphaned.

Current behavior:
- --force overwrites the hook attachment
- Previous work becomes orphaned (still assigned but not pinned)

Desired behavior:
1. Unpin the displaced molecule (clear assignee, set pinned=false)
2. Print warning in tool output: 'Warning: displaced gt-xxx back to ready pool'
3. Proceed with new sling

This ensures:
- No silent data loss
- Agent sees the warning and can act on it
- Human caller sees what happened
- Displaced work is discoverable via 'bd ready'

Implementation location: checkHookCollision() or the sling handlers themselves (after the --force check passes)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T16:00:23.713733-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T16:07:14.482319-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T16:07:14.482319-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}
